prompts.text:
Which organelle is the site of photosynthesis?
A) Mitochondria
B) Ribosome
C) Chloroplast
D) Nucleus
Answer: A/B/C/D only.

What is the capital of France?
A) Berlin
B) Madrid
C) Paris
D) Rome
Answer: A/B/C/D only.

When price increases, quantity supplied typically (ceteris paribus):
A) decreases
B) increases
C) stays the same
D) becomes zero
Answer: A/B/C/D only.

Who wrote 'The Republic'?
A) Aristotle
B) Plato
C) Socrates
D) Kant
Answer: A/B/C/D only.

Scurvy is caused by deficiency of:
A) Vitamin A
B) Vitamin B12
C) Vitamin C
D) Vitamin D
Answer: A/B/C/D only.
State Newton's second law in one sentence and give the formula.
At standard pressure, at what Celsius temperature does water boil?
What is the main function of mitochondria? One or two sentences.
In which year did World War II end?
What is the capital of Australia?

ingest_clean: Photosynthesis converts carbon dioxide and water into glucose using sunlight in chloroplasts.
World War Two ended in 1945 with Germany's unconditional surrender.
The capital city of Australia is Canberra not Sydney or Melbourne.
Newton's second law states force equals mass times acceleration F equals m a.
DNA uses four nucleotide bases adenine thymine guanine and cytosine.
Supply increases when price rises according to basic microeconomic theory.
Binary search requires a sorted array and halves the search interval each step.
Scurvy is caused by deficiency of vitamin C also called ascorbic acid.
The Yangtze River originates from glaciers on the Tibetan Plateau in China.
William Shakespeare wrote Romeo and Juliet and Hamlet in Elizabethan England.
Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean covering more area than all land combined.
Python list comprehension builds lists from iterables with optional filters.
The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille.
Mitochondria produce ATP through cellular respiration in eukaryotes.
Euler's formula e to the i pi plus one equals zero links five constants.
Inflation means a sustained increase in the general price level of goods.
Mercury is the smallest planet and closest to the Sun in our solar system.
George Washington was the first President of the United States.
GDP measures the total value of goods and services produced in a country.
光合作用在叶绿体中将二氧化碳和水转化为葡萄糖。
1945年8月日本宣布投降标志二战结束。
秦朝是中国第一个统一的中央集权封建王朝。
勾股定理：直角三角形斜边平方等于两直角边平方之和。
The SI unit of force is the Newton defined as kg times m per s squared.
Electrons carry negative charge and orbit the atomic nucleus.
Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll for capturing light energy.
Binary search runs in O log n time on sorted arrays.
Plato wrote The Republic describing an ideal state and philosopher kings.
Type 2 diabetes involves insulin resistance and relative insulin deficiency.
The Nile River in Africa is traditionally cited as the longest river.
Thomas Jefferson was primary author of the US Declaration of Independence.
George Orwell wrote the dystopian novel Nineteen Eighty-Four published in 1949.
There are twenty four solar terms in the traditional Chinese calendar.
Cao Xueqin is credited as author of the novel Dream of the Red Chamber.
Contrapositive of P implies Q is not Q implies not P.
Whales are mammals and must surface to breathe air through lungs.
Paris is the capital and largest city of France on the River Seine.
Aristotle was student of Plato and tutor of Alexander the Great.
Central Processing Unit executes instructions in a computer system.
Gross Domestic Product sums consumption investment government and net exports.
CPU memory and storage are core hardware components of computers.
Supply and demand curves intersect at market equilibrium price.
Vitamin C deficiency causes scurvy with bleeding gums and weakness.
World War II involved Allied and Axis powers from 1939 to 1945.
Canberra was selected as Australian capital as compromise between Sydney and Melbourne.
Mercury orbits closest to the Sun with year of about eighty eight Earth days.
红楼梦是中国古典小说巅峰之作作者一般认为是曹雪芹。
二十四节气反映中国古代农历对太阳周年的划分。
